author: @TechAmalgamator
text:
(EDIT: turns out the bike is 250Watt and as such is legal)

Wait "IS THIS BIKE EVEN LEGAL IN THE UK"  if a jobs worth starts asking.

What counts as an EAPC (FOR UK GOV)

An EAPC must have pedals that can be used to propel it.
It must show either:
    the power output
    the manufacturer of the motor

It must also show either:
    the battery’s voltage
    the maximum speed of the bike

Its electric motor:
    must have a maximum power output of 250 watts  ◄◄◄◄◄◄◄◄
    should not be able to propel the bike when it’s travelling more than 15.5mph

author: @zx8401ztv
text:
It sounds like you enjoyed that bike no end :-D
My mate bought a amazon swifty bike and he has to peddle that too, but no real effort.

i suppose 700 quid was a bit cheap, i expected some cost cutting.

author: @keithking1985
text: UNLOCK IT TO 35Km PER HOUR>>>   HOLD THE  BIKE AND TURN THE KEY WAIT 3 SECONDS AND BINGO> TO PUT BACK TO 25 Km PER HOUR (TOP SPEED) HOLD THE THROTTLE PLUS THE BRAKE AND TURN ON THE KEY WAIT 3 SECONDS. AND ITS BACK AGAIN.

author: @keithking1985
text: P.S. lads there is a load of these bikes that are identical, except they put different names on them ( and cost, which is crazy) two mistake but they are forgiveable, 1, be careful opening up so the pedels are out of the way of the wiring going under the bike.2, mine dose not clock speed when not using the motor.(so not sure if yours will.)

author: @keithking1985
text: i have the very same one as that. except my bike is called "SAMEBIKE" it but it's meant to be said SAM-E,BIKE.  best money i have ever spent. your going to enjoy it lads. VERY MUCH. IV ALREADY PUT UP 1,870 Km ON MINE< WITHOUT ANY PROBLEMS, (EXCEPT BLOODY PUNCTURES)

author: @dexterconnolly3696
text: Hey rob I've just set up something you might find interesting. I have a 1.2v dc motor powered by a 1.2v aaa battery i connected the motors shaft directly to another dc motor but a 12v one. Not only does the small motor spin the bigger one...I tested it and read 12.5v at around 1 amp...I plan to loop the motors and remove the battery.

author: @hommerdalor6301
text:
Hi Rob.
I know you know what you are doing, but even if almost everything was mounted on the bike, you didn't mentioned that you checked that all bolts were good tight, witch needs to be done, especially the wheels and pedal crank.
Cheers.

author: @Earzone63
text:
one of my (many) 'dreams' is to leave straight from the airport on one of these - no expensive taxi when flying on a cheaper night flight .
But for piece of mind i would pack it in a case for the hold luggage, and better if it's collapsable  ... so a folding case to fit a folding bike lol!!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I was thinking a case would be good for this!

replies.author: @goat-shaw
replies.text: battery on a plane like that  is a no-no usually

replies.author: @Earzone63
replies.text: @goat-shaw interesting.. just looked it up and the limit is 100Wh per unit or 160Wh per two units. These batts are usually 10-13Ah. so a 'split-able' 12V variant could possibly be allowed, but usually ebike are in one piece and 36-48V. Balls.

author: @microphotography2090
text: Hey Robert, I just posted a video tutorial on how to make a 1 amp hour zinc ion super capacitor.  Much of the design is from all the knowledge you have shared on your channel, so thank you.  The battery is a 3d design, lots of active material paste spread into carbon felt air filter.  Please let me know what you think, I don't think many homemade batteries reach the capacity I accomplished!  And it's all thanks to you!  (PS, we have talked before, my name is also Robert!)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I just went and watched it mate - that is awesome work I would like to feature this on my twitch stream if you are ok with that - this deserves many more views

replies.author: @microphotography2090
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Sounds fantastic!

author: @JaysElectronicTinkerShack
text: looks like a crakcing bit of kit i recently brought a broken whirlwind c4 which is a similar type of deal folding ebike obs it was broken so i set about it there had been a loose plug inside whcih shorted on another cable plug which wasnt used by the bike so a new controller , display , and pas sensor sorted thta as well as having to take th emotor apart as the hall sensors in the motor had blown also was very handy getting a ebike tester i didnt even know they existed lol

author: @RaperJason
text:
Be careful Robert  350 watt motor and a throttle if you say you have riden it on anything other than private land you are admiting to a motoring offence live on youtube especially if you hack it to go faster. As it stands out of the box it is classed as an electric motorcycle and needs registering taxing and an aproved motorcycle helmet.
I don't agree with the laws on these but they are the laws

replies.author: @perpetual4958
replies.text:
More like electric Moped. if it is over 4KW continuous power it would classify as a motorcycle. At least i presume UK hasn't changed rules from the  EU ones since Brexit.

They may also classify it as an speed-pedelec, stil requires license, insurance and an helmet.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate - I go into that a bit more on the next vid

replies.author: @RaperJason
replies.text:
@perpetual4958 Not sure about the clasifications I was always a 125cc rider with a car licence and cbt but anything classed as moped/motorcycle brings the same punishment if you don't have all the paperwork 3 points each for insurance,tax and no licence and then you have the fact that it is unregistered could easily loose your licence or as happened to the courier that killed someone whilst riding one your liberty
But one thing for sure it won't be classed as Pedelec 😀

replies.author: @RaperJason
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Looking forward to it mate👍  If you can get the law changed I'll vote for you as Prime Minister next time 😀 I would love my Pedalec to go a bit faster 👍

What counts as an EAPC (FOR UK GOV)

An EAPC must have pedals that can be used to propel it.

replies.author: @fender1000100
replies.text:
Listen nobody cares so long as you arent tearing up the street at 30 plus miles an hour. I've been riding an A20 and Fiido L3 for a long while now. Both 350w motors. Both can go 21mph plus. No problems from anyone.

If you were on an electric motorbike that can do 30 plus mph. And you dont have a license and all the rest. Then you are likely to get pulled up and problems.

With a ebike. Once they see the pedals. They dismiss it as just another bike straight away.

replies.text: @fender1000100 Experienced  cyclist do 30mph without electric no problem
